title: Linear list scan in hot path instead of O(log n) Set lookup
source_title: DAML Performance Pattern - Data Structure Selection
description: The Whitelist template stores allowed parties in a [Party] list and uses elem for membership checks. This is O(n) per lookup. For high-frequency choices like CheckAndExecute, this causes performance degradation as the whitelist grows. Using DA.Set provides O(log n) membership checks.
recommendation: Use DA.Set (or DA.Map for key-value data) instead of lists when membership checks or lookups are performed in choice bodies. Reserve lists for ordered data that needs sequential processing.

oracle.description: Membership checks should use Set/Map data structures rather than linear list scans.
oracle.static_signal: elem/notElem called on [Party] field in choice body; field could be replaced with Set Party
oracle.symbolic_property: forall w n. size(w.allowedParties) == n => complexity(CheckAndExecute, w) == O(log(n))

notes: Performance anti-pattern. While not a correctness bug, it becomes a DoS vector on Canton when the list is large enough to cause transaction timeouts.
